skill.name: twitter-automation
skill.description: Automate Twitter/X with posting, engagement, and user management via inference.sh CLI. Apps: x/post-tweet, x/post-create (with media), x/post-like, x/post-retweet, x/dm-send, x/user-follow. Capabilities: post tweets, schedule content, like posts, retweet, send DMs, follow users, get profiles. Use for: social media automation, content scheduling, engagement bots, audience growth, X API. Triggers: twitter api, x api, tweet automation, post to twitter, twitter bot, social media automation, x automation, tweet scheduler, twitter integration, post tweet, twitter post, x post, send tweet
skill.summary: Automate Twitter/X posting, engagement, and user management via the inference.sh belt CLI
skill.author: qu-skills

security_audit.summary: This is a legitimate Twitter/X automation skill that uses the inference.sh belt CLI to interact with the Twitter API. The static scanner flagged 37 instances of 'shell backtick execution' which are false positives — they are markdown inline code formatting (e.g., `belt app run x/post-tweet`), not actual command execution. The 7 hardcoded URLs are documentation links to inference.sh and GitHub. The 2 'weak cryptographic algorithm' findings are false positives triggered by the word 'engagement' and table headers. The skill's actual behavior is invoking the belt CLI to post tweets, like, retweet, send DMs, and follow users — all expected functionality for a social media automation tool.

low_findings.title: External CLI execution (belt)
low_findings.description: The skill runs the `belt` CLI from inference.sh to interact with Twitter/X API. This is the intended functionality of the skill, not a security concern. Users must explicitly install and authenticate with belt before use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The belt CLI is a legitimate third-party tool from inference.sh. All command invocations are clearly documented examples for Twitter automation.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded documentation URLs
low_findings.description: The skill contains hardcoded URLs pointing to inference.sh documentation and GitHub raw content. These are standard documentation references, not data exfiltration endpoints.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All URLs point to the official inference.sh domain or GitHub raw content for documentation purposes. No suspicious external endpoints detected.

content.value_statement: Managing Twitter/X accounts manually is time-consuming for content creators and marketers. This skill automates posting, engagement, and user management through the inference.sh belt CLI.
actual_capabilities: Post text tweets via x/post-tweet app
Create posts with media attachments via x/post-create
Like tweets using tweet IDs via x/post-like
Follow users by username via x/user-follow
Get tweet details by ID via x/post-get
Generate and post AI images via falai/flux-dev-lora workflow
Generate and post AI videos via google/veo-3-1-fast workflow
limitations: Requires inference.sh belt CLI installation and authentication
Subject to Twitter/X API rate limits and terms of service
No built-in content scheduling — requires external cron or workflow orchestration
Cannot perform advanced actions like threads, polls, or spaces via documented apps

use_cases.title: Content Creator Scheduling
use_cases.description: Automate posting of pre-written tweets and media content to maintain a consistent posting schedule without manual intervention.
use_cases.target_user: Social media managers and content creators

use_cases.title: AI Art Distribution
use_cases.description: Generate AI images with falai/flux-dev-lora and automatically post them to Twitter with captions, streamlining creative workflows.
use_cases.target_user: AI artists and generative art enthusiasts

use_cases.title: Engagement Automation
use_cases.description: Automate liking, retweeting, and following to increase account visibility and grow audience reach for brand accounts.

prompt_templates.title: Post a Simple Tweet
prompt_templates.prompt: Post a tweet that says "Just launched our new product! Check it out at example.com"

prompt_templates.title: Post with Media Attachment
prompt_templates.prompt: Post an image to Twitter with the text "New blog post is live!" using the image at https://example.com/image.jpg

prompt_templates.title: Generate and Post AI Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an AI image of a cyberpunk cityscape and post it to Twitter with the caption "AI art of the day"

prompt_templates.title: Engagement Batch Operations
prompt_templates.prompt: Like tweet ID 1234567890, retweet it, and then follow user @example

output_examples.input: Post a tweet saying "Hello from inference.sh!"
output_examples.output:
Command executed: belt app run x/post-tweet --input '{"text": "Hello from inference.sh!"}'

Result: Tweet posted successfully. Tweet ID: 1234567890123456789

Command executed: belt app run x/user-get --input '{"username": "OpenAI"}'

Result: User profile retrieved — Display name: OpenAI, Followers: 2,100,000, Bio: "AI research and deployment company."

best_practices: Always authenticate with belt login before running automation commands
Respect Twitter/X rate limits by spacing out automated actions
Review generated content before posting to maintain brand voice
Use meaningful tweet IDs and usernames to avoid errors in batch operations
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for spam or mass-following — it violates Twitter/X Terms of Service
Avoid posting identical content repeatedly — this triggers platform spam detection
Do not bypass belt CLI to call Twitter/X API directly — the CLI handles authentication and rate limiting

faq.question: What is the belt CLI?
faq.answer: The belt CLI is a command-line tool from inference.sh that provides access to 250+ AI and integration apps, including Twitter/X automation.

faq.question: Do I need a Twitter/X developer account?
faq.answer: No. The belt CLI handles Twitter/X API authentication through inference.sh, so you do not need your own developer credentials.

faq.question: Can I schedule tweets in advance?
faq.answer: The skill does not include built-in scheduling. You need to use external tools like cron jobs or workflow orchestrators to schedule belt commands.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to use?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill only invokes the belt CLI, which is an official inference.sh tool. No credentials are stored or transmitted by the skill itself.

faq.question: What AI models can generate images for posting?
faq.answer: The documentation shows falai/flux-dev-lora for images and google/veo-3-1-fast for videos, but any belt-compatible generation app can be used.

faq.question: Does this work with Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as listed in the supported tools.
